title: Is there an app to create something similar to a Google Doc?
excerpt:
Hello, 
I’m trying to look for some sort of app or integration for Monday.com where we can share something with our client to submit information, and have them able to update this information live whenever they want. For…

posts.author: jonathanhenry (Jonathan Henry)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hello,

I’m trying to look for some sort of app or integration for Monday.com where we can share something with our client to submit information, and have them able to update this information live whenever they want. For reference, currently we share a google doc with our clients to collect information on events they’re looking to hold, and it works well as they can go in and update the document as and when they need.

I’ve tried to set up forms in Monday.com for our clients to complete, however the issue is once they’ve submitted the form they aren’t then able to update the form.

Every app I’ve looked at within the monday.com app marketplace only allows you to share things with members in your team, or to create guest accounts. If we were to create guest accounts they would then be able to edit and change information for all forms submitted to that board, I’m just looking for them to be able to edit their own form.

Does anyone have any ideas?

Many Thanks,
Jon

posts.author: anon29275264 (anon29275264)
posts.cookedHtml: Isn’t this one of the use cases for monday workdocs? Though they’d need to be guests in the account and you’d need to share it with them. (guests can only see things shared with them)

posts.author: jonathanhenry (Jonathan Henry)
posts.cookedHtml:
Yes, but the issue I found with that is we work with hundreds of clients each with hundreds of different project managers submitting hundreds of different events. So we’d be charged an arm and a leg for every project manager we invite as a guest right, as well as thousands of docs being submitted and not very organisable within a workspace

This is why I looked at forms instead, as it allows us to just share the form link with our clients, no guest accounts needed, but it lacks the live element.

posts.author: banana (Ian H)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hey Jonathan, could you please help me understand your problem.

If you’re already using google docs, couldn’t you embed the google doc (using this app on the board you’re using to track your clients events?

I’m assuming you tried this already since you looked through the marketplace. Could you give me an example workflow, or a more detailed use case for the problem you’re facing.

posts.author: shayan.moussawi (Shayan Moussawi)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hello Jonathan,

Just letting you know that on Pro and Enterprise Plans the amount of Guests you can invite to your account should be unlimited.

posts.author: sachinkarma (sachin kumar karma)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hey Jon,

To address your need for clients to update their information live on Monday.com, we can leverage the integration of Google Sheets with Monday.com. By setting up a sync between a Google Sheet and your Monday.com board, clients can update their information directly in the Google Sheet, and these updates will reflect in Monday.com in real time.

Here’s a practical approach:

  • Google Sheets Integration: Connect a Google Sheet to Monday.com, allowing real-time data syncing.
  • Shared Google Sheet: Share a Google Sheet with clients for them to input and update their event details.
  • Automation: Set up automation within Monday.com to import changes from the Google Sheet at regular intervals or in real-time.

posts.author: williamswish (william)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hello,
As per me

  • Use Monday.com’s “Form View” with custom fields and permissions tailored so clients can edit their own submissions.
  • Alternatively, integrate with external form tools like Typeform or Google Forms that allow post-submission editing.
  • Consider creating separate boards or workspaces for each client to manage privacy and editing permissions.
    I hope this will help you,
    Thank you

posts.author: Mykola (Mykola Pustovoychenko)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hello @jonathanhenry

We’ve created a tool that is specifically focused on syncing monday.com with Google Sheets and other platforms. It is called resynced.io

Please find link to the marketplace app here: https://monday.com/marketplace/listing/10000688/resyncedio

You can do a one- or two-way sync, so that whenever you update anything in your monday.com board, it updated on the Google Sheets spreadsheet that is shared with your client. If you want, it can also work in other direction: if your client changes something in their spreadsheet, it will automatically be updated in your board. You can control which columns you’d like to share.

The setup is simple, and you may start having your data in sync within few minutes.
